channel.title: AI on Charlotte's AI Lab
channel.description: Recent content in AI on Charlotte's AI Lab

item.title: The Biggest Irony Since Steve Jobs Left: Apple Now Needs Google
item.description: Apple is paying Google $1 billion a year for Gemini to power Siri — the same Siri that once mocked Google Assistant

item.title: 75% of Resumes Get Trashed by AI Before Any Human Sees Them
item.description: 75% of resumes get rejected by AI before any human HR ever sees them. You sent out 100 applications with zero callbacks? Nobody actually read your resume. Here’s how ATS screening works, why the AI-vs-AI resume arms race is absurd, and what you can actually do about it.

item.title: 1 Million Tokens: Claude Can Now Remember an Entire Book
item.description:
One million tokens.

I checked this number several times, went to Claude’s official announcement to verify, and only then opened my laptop to write this.

On March 13, Anthropic officially announced: the 1 million token context window for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 is now generally available. Not beta, not a waitlist, not a “gradual rollout for select users” — it’s GA, available to everyone.

Let me start with what this number actually means.

item.title: One Man, One Dog, Two AI Tools: The Cancer Story That Broke the Internet
item.description:
My X timeline got flooded these past couple of days with a single tweet.

“Australian programmer, zero biology background, used ChatGPT and AlphaFold to design an mRNA cancer vaccine from scratch and saved his dog.”

Over 70,000 likes. 8 million impressions.

Demis Hassabis personally retweeted it saying “This is just the beginning of digital biology.” Greg Brockman also shared it.

Honestly, when I first saw it, I felt it too. One person, one dog, two AI tools, taking on cancer.

item.title: Raised $1 Billion — Yann LeCun Is Betting That LLMs Are a Dead End
item.description: Turing Award winner Yann LeCun left Meta to found AMI Labs, raised $1 billion, and declared LLMs a dead end. His bet: “World Models” will redefine AI.

item.title: When AI Makes Every Skill Cheap, What Do We Have Left?
item.description: Reflections on Dan Koe’s ‘The Future of Work’ — redefining personal value in the age of AI
